name: Metatron
description: Metatron is a prominent figure in Jewish mysticism and some branches of Christianity, particularly within Kabbalah. He is not mentioned in the Hebrew Bible directly, but his role developed in post-biblical Jewish literature, particularly in the Enoch literature and the Kabbalistic tradition. Metatron is often described as the highest of the angels, serving as a celestial scribe, the "lesser YHWH," and the prince of the divine presence. His origin is debated; some traditions identify him with the biblical Enoch, who was transformed into an angel after ascending to heaven. He is seen as a mediator between God and humanity, possessing immense power and wisdom. Different texts attribute various functions and attributes to Metatron, including guiding souls, recording human actions, and representing the divine will. He is a complex figure whose significance has evolved over centuries of religious and esoteric thought, making him a crucial subject for understanding the development of Abrahamic angelology.
subtitle: The Prince of the Divine Presence

keyMyths.title: The Transformation of Enoch
keyMyths.description: One of the most prominent myths surrounding Metatron is his identification with the biblical figure Enoch. According to this tradition, Enoch, known for his righteousness, was taken by God and transformed into the angel Metatron. This transformation imbued him with immense wisdom and power, elevating him to a position second only to God. This narrative highlights the potential for human ascension and the attainment of divine status through piety and service.

keyMyths.title: Metatron as the Lesser YHWH
keyMyths.description: In some Kabbalistic texts, Metatron is referred to as the "lesser YHWH," signifying his unique connection to the divine. This title suggests that Metatron embodies aspects of God's presence and acts as a conduit for divine power. However, this identification has been subject to debate, with some scholars arguing that it should not be interpreted as literal divinity but rather as a symbolic representation of Metatron's elevated status and function within the celestial realm. The concept underscores his role as an intermediary between God and creation.

keyMyths.title: Metatron's Role in Guiding Souls
keyMyths.description: Metatron is also depicted as a guide for souls, both in this life and the afterlife. He helps souls navigate the complexities of the spiritual realms and facilitates their journey towards enlightenment. This function emphasizes Metatron's compassionate nature and his commitment to assisting humanity in its spiritual evolution. His role as a soul guide connects him to themes of redemption, salvation, and the ultimate destiny of human beings.

sources.author: Scholem, Gershom

sources.text: Provides modern perspectives on the Kabbalah, including analysis of Metatron's role within the Kabbalistic system and its symbolism.

associations.name: White
associations.significance: Represents purity, divine light, and Metatron's connection to the celestial realm.
Associated with the 72 names of God and Metatron's role in divine administration.

associations.name: Merkabah
associations.significance: Metatron is closely linked to the Merkabah, the divine chariot, symbolizing his role as a guide to higher spiritual realms.

associations.name: Divine Scribe
associations.significance: Reflects Metatron's duty to record the deeds of humanity and maintain the celestial records.

associations.name: The Lesser YHWH
associations.significance: Indicates Metatron's high status and his close proximity to the divine presence.

associations.name: Fire
associations.significance: Symbolizes transformation, purification, and the intense energy of the divine.

cross_cultural_parallels.similarity: Both serve as messengers of the gods and guides of souls.
Shared role as a divine scribe and keeper of knowledge.
Similar function as a record-keeper of human actions for divine judgment.

worshipPractices.name: Meditation
worshipPractices.description: Some Kabbalists meditate on Metatron's name or sigil to seek guidance and spiritual insight.

worshipPractices.name: Invocation
worshipPractices.description: In some traditions, Metatron is invoked for protection, healing, and assistance in spiritual development.

cultural.modernLegacy: Metatron remains a popular figure in modern occultism, New Age spirituality, and angel therapy. He is often invoked for healing, guidance, and spiritual transformation. His name and sigils are used in various practices aimed at connecting with higher realms of consciousness.

extendedContent.title: Origins & Birth
extendedContent.content: Metatron's origins are shrouded in mystery, as he is not mentioned in the Hebrew Bible. He emerges in post-biblical Jewish literature, particularly the apocryphal Book of Enoch. In some accounts, Metatron is identified with Enoch, the biblical figure who was taken by God. This tradition suggests that Enoch was transformed into the angel Metatron after ascending to heaven due to his righteousness. Other accounts depict Metatron as a primordial angel created at the beginning of time, holding a unique position in the celestial hierarchy. Regardless of the specific origin story, Metatron's rise to prominence reflects a growing interest in angelology and mystical speculation within Jewish thought. The development of his character demonstrates an effort to bridge the gap between the human and the divine, offering a figure who embodies both human potential and divine authority. The concept of a human transforming into an angel also highlights themes of spiritual ascent and the possibility of achieving divine status through righteous living.

extendedContent.title: Major Myths & Stories
extendedContent.content: While not featuring in traditional narratives, Metatron's 'myths' are primarily found in Kabbalistic texts and mystical traditions. One prominent story recounts how Metatron interceded on behalf of humanity before God, arguing for their redemption. Another tale describes Metatron's role as a guide for Moses during his ascent to Mount Sinai, imparting divine wisdom and knowledge. A central theme in these stories is Metatron's position as the 'lesser YHWH,' a title that signifies his proximity to God and his delegated authority. However, this title also led to controversy, with some early Jewish scholars questioning whether it implied a belief in two divine beings. Metatron is often depicted as a celestial scribe, recording the deeds of humans and maintaining the divine records. He is also associated with the Merkabah, the divine chariot, serving as a guide to those who seek to ascend to higher spiritual realms. These stories emphasize Metatron's role as a mediator, a guide, and a protector of humanity, highlighting his importance in the Kabbalistic worldview.

extendedContent.title: Legacy & Influence
extendedContent.content: Metatron's legacy extends beyond Jewish mysticism, influencing various esoteric and New Age movements. His image as a powerful and benevolent angel resonates with those seeking spiritual guidance and healing. Metatron's Cube, a geometric figure derived from Kabbalistic teachings, is often used as a symbol for meditation and energy work. The cube is believed to contain all the platonic solids, representing the fundamental building blocks of the universe. In modern angel therapy, Metatron is invoked for clearing negative energy, promoting spiritual growth, and connecting with one's higher self. His teachings are often associated with the concept of ascension and the raising of one's vibrational frequency. Metatron's enduring appeal lies in his ability to bridge the gap between the human and the divine, offering a path towards spiritual enlightenment and personal transformation. He continues to inspire artists, writers, and spiritual seekers who are drawn to his message of hope, healing, and divine connection. His presence in popular culture is a testament to the ongoing fascination with angels and the mystical realms.

shortDescription: A high-ranking angel in Abrahamic lore, particularly Kabbalah. He is the celestial scribe and 'lesser YHWH', serving as a mediator between God and humanity.
